VFO_A_lcdNumber:
Frequency setting of the VFO-A register in Hz.

The software keeps frequency values within the amateur bands:

1.8 MHz - 54.0 MHz
144 MHz - 148 MHz
430 MHz - 450 MHz

For extended receive operating range, start the program with the --exop parameter.
VFO_A_lineEdit:
Entry box for manual setting of the VFO-A frequency.

Enter the frequency using the keyboard in units of MHz.

You can use a + or - sign before the number for relative increments or decrements. For example, entering +0.005 increases the current frequency by 5 kHz.

If no sign is used, the value is interpreted as an absolute frequency. For example, entering 28.4 sets the frequency to 28.4 MHz.
VFO_A_dial:
Frequency dial for the VFO-A register.

Right-click on the dial to set focus.

Cursor up/down keys: change frequency in 10 Hz steps

Page up/down keys: change frequency in 100 Hz steps

FAST: multiplies the frequency step size by 100.
VFO_B_lcdNumber:
Frequency setting of the VFO-B register in Hz.

The software keeps frequency values within the amateur bands:

1.8 MHz - 54.0 MHz
144 MHz - 148 MHz
430 MHz - 450 MHz

For extended receive operating range, start the program with the --exop parameter.
VFO_B_lineEdit:
Entry box for manual setting of the VFO-B frequency.

Enter the frequency using the keyboard in units of MHz.

You can use a + or - sign before the number for relative increments or decrements. For example, entering +0.005 increases the current frequency by 5 kHz.

If no sign is used, the value is interpreted as an absolute frequency. For example, entering 28.4 sets the frequency to 28.4 MHz.
VFO_B_dial:
Frequency dial for the VFO-B register.

Right-click on the dial to set focus.

Cursor up/down keys: change frequency in 10 Hz steps

Page up/down keys: change frequency in 100 Hz steps

FAST: multiplies the frequency step size by 100.
Clar_dial:
Clarifier shift dial.

Right-click on the dial to set focus.

Cursor up/down keys: change CLAR shift in 10 Hz steps

Page up/down keys: change CLAR shift in 100 Hz steps
Memory_groupBox:
Memory channel table.

Select a channel by clicking on a table row.

V/M: Switches to memory mode using the selected channel. Once in memory mode, channels can be changed by clicking table rows or by using keyboard arrow keys.

A→M: Saves the current VFO-A register settings into the selected memory channel.

Up/Down/Stop: Frequency scan controls. If the radio is in memory mode, memory scanning is performed. If the radio is in VFO mode, VFO scanning is performed.
IF_spectrum_frame:
Spectrum of the received signal.

FFT parameters (Rate/Gain/Size/Window) are exclusively used by the software to control the spectrum display and have no effect on radio reception.
EQ_filter_frame:
Simulated response of the microphone equalizer filter.

The filter is modeled by cascading three peaking-filter sections with the specified center frequency, gain, and Q-factor.

Displayed numbers next to the sliders represent the actual register values sent to the radio.
Audio_filter_frame:
Simulated response of the Tx audio filter.

The filter is modeled by cascading low-pass and high-pass filter sections with the specified cutoff frequencies and slope parameters. The slope parameter determines the filter order.

Displayed numbers next to the sliders represent the actual register values sent to the radio.
Message_textBrowser:
COM traffic between the software and the radio.

The text can be selected and copied with the mouse for debugging purposes.
Meter_groupBox:
Radio meters.

Vertical red line shows the peak value.

S: Signal strength. Displayed value can be inaccurate at very large or very small signal strengths.

PO: Transmitter output power in watts.
